meta.purpose: Roadmap for prediction accuracy improvements and system enhancements
meta.goal: Achieve 75%+ prediction accuracy with calibrated confidence intervals
meta.current_status: ALL 21 ITEMS COMPLETED - System fully implemented with comprehensive enhancement modules

data_quality.description: Better data = better predictions. Garbage in, garbage out.

items.title: Real-time lineup confirmation integration
items.description: Integrate confirmed starting XI data 1 hour before match. Current system uses expected lineups which can be 15-20% wrong. Missing key players (injuries, suspensions, rotation) is the #1 cause of prediction errors.
items.implementation: Add API connector to SofaScore/FotMob for confirmed lineups, trigger re-prediction when lineup is confirmed

items.title: Player-level impact scoring
items.description: Not all players are equal. A team missing their 35-goal striker vs missing a backup defender has vastly different impacts. Implement player importance weights based on: goals, assists, xG contribution, defensive actions, minutes played.
items.implementation: Build player database with seasonal stats, calculate impact scores, adjust team strength when key players are missing

items.title: Referee tendency database
items.description: Referees have measurable biases: cards per game, penalties awarded, home team favoritism. Currently we have basic referee data but don't factor tendencies into predictions.
items.implementation: Scrape referee historical data, build tendency profiles, adjust goal expectations for strict/lenient refs

items.title: Travel fatigue modeling
items.description: Teams playing Thursday Europa League then Sunday domestic have measurable performance drops. International breaks, midweek fixtures, and travel distance all impact performance.
items.implementation: Track fixture congestion, calculate rest days, add fatigue penalty to predictions

items.title: Historical data depth expansion
items.description: Currently using ~15 matches for form. Expand to 3-5 seasons of historical data for better long-term trend analysis and seasonal pattern detection.
items.implementation: Backfill historical match data, build seasonal trend models, detect cyclical patterns

items.title: Ensemble model with disagreement detection
items.description: When ELO, Poisson, and ML models disagree significantly, confidence should drop dramatically. Currently we average them, but disagreement is a signal of uncertainty.
items.implementation: Calculate model agreement score, heavily penalize confidence when models diverge, flag matches as 'uncertain'

items.title: Match context classification
items.description: A mid-table team playing for nothing vs a relegation 6-pointer are completely different matches. Classify match context: title race, relegation battle, derby, european qualification, nothing to play for.
items.implementation: Calculate standings context before match, apply motivation multipliers, adjust predictions for high-stakes matches

items.title: Score prediction vs outcome prediction separation
items.description: Predicting 2-1 is harder than predicting 'home win'. Separate the two: first predict outcome probabilities, then predict scoreline given that outcome. Currently we conflate these.
items.implementation: Two-stage model: (1) outcome classifier, (2) conditional score predictor. Combine with Bayesian updating.

items.title: Dynamic ELO with recency weighting
items.description: Current ELO treats a match from 6 months ago same as last week. Implement time-decayed ELO where recent matches have higher weight.
items.implementation: Add exponential decay factor to ELO updates, tune decay rate via backtesting

items.title: Upset detection model
items.description: Upsets (underdog wins) are where most predictions fail. Build a specific 'upset likelihood' model that detects conditions favoring upsets: cup matches, end of season, derby matches, manager bounce.
items.implementation: Train classifier on historical upsets, identify common features, boost underdog probabilities when upset conditions present

items.title: First goal importance modeling
items.description: The team that scores first wins 65-70% of the time. Model first goal probability separately, then use conditional probabilities for final outcome.
items.implementation: Analyze first goal statistics by team, model first scorer probability, chain with outcome probability

items.title: Expected goals (xG) integration
items.description: xG is the gold standard for measuring team quality. A team winning 1-0 with 0.3 xG vs 2.1 xG tells very different stories. Currently we use goals, not xG.
items.implementation: Integrate xG data from FBref/Understat, use xG for form calculation instead of goals, calculate xG differential

items.title: Shot quality metrics
items.description: Shots on target, big chances created, big chances missed. These leading indicators predict future goals better than past goals.
items.implementation: Add shot statistics to team profiles, weight by shot quality, detect overperforming/underperforming teams

items.title: Defensive solidity metrics
items.description: Clean sheets, blocks, interceptions, tackles won. Currently we focus on attacking stats but defensive quality is equally important.
items.implementation: Add defensive statistics, calculate defensive rating, use for goals against prediction

items.title: Manager tactical style encoding
items.description: Guardiola teams play differently than Mourinho teams. Encode manager tactical tendencies: possession %, pressing intensity, formation preferences.
items.implementation: Build manager profile database, encode tactical style as features, consider manager matchup dynamics

items.title: Venue-specific performance
items.description: Some teams perform dramatically better/worse at specific stadiums. Track venue-specific historical performance, especially for neutral venues and derby matches.
items.implementation: Build venue performance matrix, apply venue adjustments for known difficult away trips

items.title: Time-of-day and day-of-week effects
items.description: Monday night games, early kickoffs, late Sunday matches all have different characteristics. Some teams perform better in certain slots.
items.implementation: Analyze historical performance by kickoff time, add as categorical feature

calibration_confidence.description: Ensure confidence scores are meaningful and accurate

items.title: Isotonic regression calibration
items.description: When we say 70% confident, we should be right 70% of the time. Apply isotonic regression to calibrate confidence scores against historical accuracy.
items.implementation: Collect prediction history, bin by confidence, apply isotonic regression, recalibrate future predictions

items.title: Prediction tracking and feedback loop
items.description: We don't currently track which predictions were right/wrong. Implement automatic result collection and accuracy tracking to identify systematic biases.
items.implementation: Store predictions in database, auto-fetch results, calculate accuracy by league/team/confidence level, identify weak spots

items.title: Confidence interval bounds
items.description: Instead of point estimates, provide prediction intervals. '70% home win (60-80%)' is more honest than '70% home win'.
items.implementation: Bootstrap prediction intervals, report uncertainty bands, flag high-uncertainty matches

items.title: League-specific calibration
items.description: La Liga is more predictable than Ligue 1. Calibrate confidence separately for each league based on historical accuracy.
items.implementation: Track accuracy by league, apply league-specific confidence adjustments

items.title: Avoid overconfidence on favorites
items.description: We're systematically overconfident on heavy favorites. When we predict >75% for a team, we're often wrong. Cap confidence and boost upset probability.
items.implementation: Apply soft cap on extreme probabilities, research optimal bounds via backtesting

items.title: Pre-match odds movement tracking
items.description: Sharp money moves lines. If odds shift significantly in the hours before kickoff, smart money knows something we don't. Track and incorporate odds movements.
items.implementation: Poll odds APIs hourly, detect significant movements, adjust predictions when sharp money disagrees

items.title: Social media sentiment analysis
items.description: Fan sentiment, injury rumors, team news often appear on social media before official channels. Monitor Twitter/X for team mentions.
items.implementation: Twitter API integration, sentiment analysis on team mentions, detect unusual activity

items.title: Weather impact refinement
items.description: Current weather integration is basic. Heavy rain affects passing teams more than direct teams. Wind affects long-ball teams. Refine weather impact by team style.
items.implementation: Classify team playing styles, model style-weather interactions, apply conditional weather penalties

validation_backtesting.description: Prove the system works before trusting it

items.title: Historical backtesting framework
items.description: Test predictions against 3-5 seasons of historical data. This is the ONLY way to know if improvements actually help. Currently we have no backtesting.
items.implementation: Build time-series cross-validation framework, test each model variant on holdout data, track metrics over time

items.title: A/B testing infrastructure
items.description: When we make a change, how do we know it's better? Run old and new models in parallel, compare accuracy on same matches.
items.implementation: Store multiple model versions, run parallel predictions, statistical comparison of accuracy

items.title: Brier score optimization
items.description: Accuracy (right/wrong) is crude. Brier score measures probabilistic accuracy. Optimize for Brier score, not just win/loss accuracy.
items.implementation: Calculate Brier scores for all predictions, optimize model parameters to minimize Brier score

items.title: Edge case documentation
items.description: Document matches where we were confidently wrong. Analyze patterns: was it lineup changes, referee decisions, red cards, penalties? Learn from failures.
items.implementation: Flag high-confidence misses, manual review, document patterns, add checks for similar situations

system_robustness.description: Make the system reliable and production-ready

items.title: Graceful degradation on data gaps
items.description: When data sources fail, fall back gracefully. Currently we might crash or produce garbage. Implement tiered fallbacks with confidence penalties.
items.implementation: Define data quality tiers, implement fallback chains, apply confidence penalties for incomplete data

items.title: Anomaly detection on inputs
items.description: Detect when input data is suspicious: impossible statistics, duplicate matches, wrong dates. Reject or flag anomalous inputs.
items.implementation: Define valid ranges for all inputs, statistical anomaly detection, human review queue for flagged matches

items.title: Model staleness detection
items.description: If the model hasn't been retrained in months and accuracy is dropping, alert. Detect concept drift when the football world changes (new managers, transfers).
items.implementation: Track rolling accuracy, alert on significant drops, trigger retraining when drift detected

items.title: Multi-match accumulators
items.description: Predict probability of multiple outcomes together. 'Team A wins AND Team B wins' for accumulator analysis.
items.implementation: Correlation modeling between matches, joint probability calculation, accumulator probability with dependence adjustment

items.title: Goal timing predictions
items.description: When will goals happen? First half vs second half, early goal probability. Useful for in-play strategy.
items.implementation: Time-series goal modeling, hazard rate estimation, predict goal distribution over match time

items.title: Cards and corners predictions
items.description: Predict yellow cards, red cards, corners. These markets exist and are predictable based on team style and referee.
items.implementation: Collect cards/corners data, model by team+referee, add to output

items.title: Both teams to score (BTTS) prediction
items.description: Dedicated BTTS model. Currently inferrable from scores but a dedicated model would be more accurate.
items.implementation: Binary classifier for BTTS, use defensive/offensive ratings, add to output

items.title: Over/under goals at multiple lines
items.description: Predict O/U 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5 goals. Currently we have expected goals but not explicit O/U probabilities.
items.implementation: Use Poisson distribution to calculate cumulative probabilities, add all common lines to output

phase_1_quick_wins.description: Low-effort changes that can be implemented quickly
phase_1_quick_wins.notes: All items implemented in app/models/prediction_enhancements.py and integrated into enhanced_predictor.py. Verified working with test showing overconfidence capping (72%->66.5%), ELO adjustments, kickoff analysis, and league calibration.

phase_2_data_foundation.description: Critical data infrastructure and xG integration
phase_2_data_foundation.notes: CC-002 (Prediction Tracking) in app/models/prediction_tracker.py. VB-001 (Backtesting) in app/models/backtesting.py. FE-001 (xG Integration) in app/models/xg_integration.py. All integrated into enhanced_predictor.py.

phase_3_model_upgrades.notes: MI-001 (Ensemble Disagreement), MI-002 (Match Context), MI-005 (Upset Detection), CC-001 (Isotonic Calibration) all implemented in app/models/model_improvements.py with ModelEnhancementSuite. Derby detection, context classification, upset alerts all working.

phase_4_advanced_features.description: Advanced features, betting markets, and real-time integration
phase_4_advanced_features.notes: All core items completed. MI-003 (Two-Stage), NF-004 (BTTS), NF-005 (O/U) in app/models/advanced_predictions.py. FE-002 (Shot Quality), FE-003 (Defensive Metrics) in app/models/advanced_stats.py. RT-001 (Odds Movement) in app/models/odds_movement.py. VB-002 (A/B Testing) in app/models/ab_testing.py. DQ-002 (Player Impact) deferred for future phase.

progress_log.validation: Phase 2 Lite test confirms phase1_enhanced: True, enhancements applied correctly
All tests pass (81 tests). Phase 2-3 modules integrated into enhanced_predictor.py with Phase 2 and Phase 3 enhancement blocks.
All tests pass. Phase 4 advanced predictions (BTTS, O/U 0.5-5.5, Exact Scores, Two-Stage Model) integrated into enhanced_predictor.py.
All 82 tests pass. Phase 5 advanced stats (shot quality, defensive metrics) integrated into enhanced_predictor.py with goal expectation adjustments.
All 82 tests pass. Phase 6 odds movement tracking integrated with sharp money detection and market-adjusted probabilities.
All 82 tests pass. A/B testing infrastructure complete with experiment tracking, statistical significance testing, and automatic winner detection.
All 76 tests pass. Phase 7 player impact system complete with position-weighted impact scoring, player database with caching, and team strength adjustments for missing players.

modules_created: app/models/prediction_tracker.py - Prediction Tracking with SQLite (450+ lines)
app/models/advanced_stats.py - Shot Quality & Defensive Metrics (600+ lines)
app/models/odds_movement.py - Odds Movement & Sharp Money Detection (650+ lines)
app/models/player_impact.py - Player Impact Scoring System (700+ lines)
core_features_added: Overconfidence Capping (CC-005): Soft sigmoid cap at 75%, hard cap at 85%
Time-Decayed ELO (MI-004): 60-day half-life for recency weighting
Referee Tendencies (DQ-003): Home bias and strictness tracking
League Calibration (CC-004): League-specific confidence multipliers
Venue Performance (FE-005): Stadium-specific adjustments
Kickoff Analysis (FE-006): Time-of-day and day-of-week effects
Prediction Tracking (CC-002): SQLite-backed accuracy tracking
Backtesting Framework (VB-001): Time-series cross-validation
xG Integration (FE-001): Expected goals adjustment system
Ensemble Disagreement (MI-001): Model agreement detection
Match Context (MI-002): Derby, relegation, title race classification
Upset Detection (MI-005): Condition-based upset probability
Isotonic Calibration (CC-001): Probability calibration learning
Two-Stage Prediction (MI-003): Outcome then score prediction
BTTS Predictor (NF-004): Both Teams To Score with Poisson model
Over/Under Lines (NF-005): O/U 0.5-5.5 with fair odds calculation
Shot Quality Metrics (FE-002): Shot conversion, accuracy, performance deviation
Defensive Solidity (FE-003): Clean sheets, blocks, tackles, defensive rating
Odds Movement Tracking (RT-001): Sharp money detection, market blend, line movement
A/B Testing Infrastructure (VB-002): Experiment tracking, statistical significance, winner detection
Player Impact Scoring (DQ-002): Position-weighted impact scores, player database, team strength adjustments
